expectations.text: Uses three-tier scope (MUST HAVE / SHOULD HAVE / OUT OF SCOPE)
expectations.evidence: No formal scope tiers. Has a 'Step 7: Polish (stretch goals)' but no MUST HAVE / SHOULD HAVE / OUT OF SCOPE structure.

expectations.text: Creates GDD.md separate from CLAUDE.md (two distinct documents)
expectations.evidence: Never mentions GDD or game design document. No mention of CLAUDE.md as a separate deliverable.

expectations.text: Mentions design pillars or core loop concept
expectations.evidence: No mention of design pillars or core loop anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Mentions delegating to subagents for heavy work
expectations.evidence: No mention of subagents or delegation.

expectations.text: Specifies incremental build order with testing after each entity
expectations.evidence: Clear 7-step build order, each step has 'Test:' instructions (e.g., 'Test: player moves around an empty world')

expectations.text: Includes anti-patterns from real sessions (body collision, area_entered, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Mentions hitbox/hurtbox pattern, periodic overlap checks, area_entered limitation, PROCESS_MODE_ALWAYS for dialog

expectations.text: Asks user to name the game
expectations.evidence: 'what would you like to name the project'

expectations.text: Shows collision layers with explicit bitmask values (not just layer numbers)
expectations.evidence: Shows collision layer table with layer numbers 1-7 but no bitmask values

expectations.text: Mentions analyzing sprite sheets before writing animation code
expectations.evidence: 'Row order is DOWN=0, LEFT=1, RIGHT=2, UP=3', frame count normalization, per-sheet hframes differences
